Title: Registration Form - Personal View
Steps:
1. Navigate to registration page
2. Select 'Personal' account type
3. Verify all form fields display
Expected Result:
- First Name field visible
- Last Name field visible
- Email field visible
- Password fields visible
- Terms checkbox present

Title: Create Account - Personal User
Steps:
1. Fill all required fields with valid data
2. Use unique email address
3. Set password meeting policy
4. Accept terms
5. Submit form
Expected Result:
- Account created successfully
- Confirmation displayed
- Can login with credentials

Title: Registration Form - Organization View
Steps:
1. Navigate to registration page
2. Select 'Organization' account type
3. Verify organization fields appear
Expected Result:
- Company Name field visible
- All personal fields visible
- Organization-specific options shown

Title: Create Account - Organization
Steps:
1. Select Organization type
2. Fill company name and details
3. Fill personal information
4. Submit form
Expected Result:
- Organization created
- User assigned as admin
- Organization visible in admin

Title: Password Visibility Toggle
Steps:
1. Enter password in password field
2. Click visibility toggle icon
3. Verify password becomes visible
4. Click toggle again
5. Verify password masked
Expected Result:
- Password toggles between masked/visible
- Works for both password fields

Title: Validation - Personal Required Fields
Steps:
1. Leave all fields empty
2. Click submit
3. Verify validation errors
4. Fill one field, submit
5. Verify remaining errors
Expected Result:
- Error shown for each empty required field
- Clear error messages
- Cannot submit until all filled

Title: Validation - Personal Max Characters
Steps:
1. Enter 256+ characters in First Name
2. Enter 256+ characters in Last Name
3. Enter very long email
4. Submit form
Expected Result:
- Max length enforced
- Appropriate error messages
- Form prevents overlong input

Title: Validation - Organization Required Fields
Steps:
1. Select Organization type
2. Leave all fields empty
3. Submit form
4. Verify all validation errors
Expected Result:
- Company name required error
- All personal field errors
- Clear messaging

Title: Validation - Unique Email
Steps:
1. Open registration form
2. Enter email of existing user
3. Fill other fields
4. Submit form
Expected Result:
- Error: Email already registered
- Suggests login or password reset

Title: Validation - Email Format
Steps:
1. Enter invalid email formats:
   - 'notanemail'
   - 'missing@domain'
   - '@nodomain.com'
2. Verify validation
Expected Result:
- Invalid format rejected
- Clear error message
- Valid format accepted

Title: Validation - Password Match
Steps:
1. Enter password in first field
2. Enter different password in confirm field
3. Submit form
Expected Result:
- Error: Passwords do not match
- Cannot submit until matching

Title: Validation - Password Policy
Steps:
1. Enter weak password: '123'
2. Verify rejection
3. Enter password without uppercase
4. Enter password without number
5. Enter valid complex password
Expected Result:
- Policy requirements enforced
- Clear feedback on requirements
- Strong password accepted

Title: Email Verification - Without
Steps:
1. Register new account
2. Verify no verification email required
3. Login immediately
Expected Result:
- Account active immediately
- No verification step
- Can login right away

Title: Email Verification - Optional
Steps:
1. Register new account
2. Verify option to verify or skip
3. Test both paths
Expected Result:
- Verification email sent
- Can skip verification
- Reminder shown if skipped

Title: Email Verification - Mandatory
Steps:
1. Register new account
2. Attempt login before verification
3. Verify email link
4. Login after verification
Expected Result:
- Cannot login before verification
- Verification link works
- Account active after verification

Title: Login - Empty Email
Steps:
1. Leave email field empty
2. Enter password
3. Click Sign In
Expected Result:
- Error: Email is required
- Cannot submit

Title: Login - Invalid User
Steps:
1. Enter non-existent email
2. Enter any password
3. Submit
Expected Result:
- Error: Invalid credentials
- No user information leaked

Title: Login - Invalid Password
Steps:
1. Enter valid email
2. Enter wrong password
3. Submit
Expected Result:
- Error: Invalid credentials
- Access failed count incremented

Title: Login - Email Verification Required
Steps:
1. Enter unverified user credentials
2. Submit login
Expected Result:
- Error: Email verification required
- Option to resend verification

Title: Login - Temporary Lockout
Steps:
1. Enter wrong password 5 times
2. Attempt login with correct password
3. Wait for lockout period
4. Retry login
Expected Result:
- Account temporarily locked after 5 failures
- Clear lockout message
- Can login after timeout

Title: Login - Admin Blocked User
Steps:
1. Attempt login with blocked user
2. Verify rejection
Expected Result:
- Error: Account is locked
- Contact admin message

Title: Login - Token Generation
Steps:
1. Login with valid credentials
2. Check /connect/token response
3. Verify token format
Expected Result:
- JWT token generated
- Token contains required claims
- Refresh token present

Title: Login - Token Persistence
Steps:
1. Note current token
2. Reload page
3. Verify still logged in
4. Check token refreshed
Expected Result:
- Session persists across reload
- Token automatically refreshed
- No re-login required

Title: Logout - Token Revocation
Steps:
1. Click Logout
2. Check /revoke/token call
3. Attempt to use old token
Expected Result:
- Logout completes
- Token revoked
- Old token rejected

Title: SSO - Entra ID Enabled
Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Verify 'Sign in with Microsoft' button
3. Click SSO button
4. Complete Microsoft auth
5. Verify redirect back
Expected Result:
- SSO button visible
- Redirects to Microsoft
- User created/linked
- Logged in successfully

Title: SSO - Google Enabled
Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Verify 'Sign in with Google' button
3. Click SSO button
4. Complete Google auth
5. Verify redirect back
Expected Result:
- SSO button visible
- Redirects to Google
- User created/linked
- Logged in successfully

Title: Login on Behalf - With Permission
Preconditions: Admin user with 'login on behalf' permission
Steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to user management
3. Select target user
4. Click 'Login on behalf'
5. Verify context switch
Expected Result:
- Admin can impersonate user
- User context active
- Can return to admin

Title: Login on Behalf - Org Maintainer
Steps:
1. Login as org maintainer
2. Navigate to organization members
3. Select employee
4. Login on behalf
Expected Result:
- Maintainer can impersonate employees
- Limited to own organization

Title: Account Menu - Purchasing Group Structure
Preconditions: User logged in with account access
Steps:
1. Log in to storefront
2. Navigate to account section (e.g. via user icon or My Account link)
3. Locate the left rail navigation menu
4. Verify 'Purchasing' group header is visible
5. Verify the following items appear under Purchasing: Dashboard, Orders, Lists, Saved for Later
Expected Result:
- 'Purchasing' group label visible in left rail
- Dashboard link present under Purchasing
- Orders link present under Purchasing
- Lists link present under Purchasing
- Saved for Later link present under Purchasing
- Items are in correct order

Title: Account Menu - User Group Structure
Steps:
1. Navigate to account left rail menu
2. Locate 'User' group header
3. Verify the following items appear under User: Profile, Addresses, Change Password, Saved Credit Cards
Expected Result:
- 'User' group label visible
- Profile link under User
- Addresses link under User
- Change Password link under User
- Saved Credit Cards link under User

Title: Account Menu - Group Headers Are Non-Clickable Labels
Preconditions: User logged in, account menu visible
Steps:
1. Navigate to account left rail menu
2. Click on the 'Purchasing' group header
3. Verify no navigation occurs
4. Click on the 'User' group header
5. Verify no navigation occurs
Expected Result:
- Group headers ('Purchasing', 'User', 'Marketing', 'Corporate') are not links
- Clicking a group header does not navigate anywhere
- Cursor style indicates non-interactive (default, not pointer)
- Headers serve as visual section labels only

Title: Account Menu - All Navigation Items Route Correctly
Preconditions: User logged in, account menu visible
Steps:
1. Click 'Dashboard' in Purchasing group, verify correct page
2. Click 'Orders' in Purchasing group, verify Order History page
3. Click 'Lists' in Purchasing group, verify Lists page
4. Click 'Saved for Later' in Purchasing group, verify Saved for Later page
5. Click 'Profile' in User group, verify Profile page
6. Click 'Addresses' in User group, verify Addresses page
7. Click 'Change Password' in User group, verify Change Password page
8. Click 'Saved Credit Cards' in User group, verify Saved Credit Cards page
Expected Result:
- Each menu item navigates to the correct page
- Page title or heading confirms correct destination
- Left rail remains visible after navigation
- Active menu item is highlighted

Title: Account Menu - Active State Highlighting
Preconditions: User logged in, navigating between account pages
Steps:
1. Navigate to Orders page via menu
2. Verify 'Orders' item is highlighted/active in left rail
3. Navigate to Profile page via menu
4. Verify 'Profile' is now highlighted and 'Orders' is no longer active
Expected Result:
- Currently viewed page item is visually highlighted (bold, border, or background)
- Only one item active at a time
- Active state updates correctly on navigation

Title: Account Menu - Mobile Responsive Layout
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 375x667
2. Navigate to account section
3. Verify left rail menu adapts for mobile (hamburger, drawer, or tabs)
4. Open mobile menu
5. Verify grouped structure visible on mobile
6. Navigate to Orders via mobile menu
Expected Result:
- Account menu accessible on mobile
- Grouped structure maintained (Purchasing, User groups visible)
- Touch targets adequate (44x44px minimum)
- Menu opens/closes correctly
- Navigation from mobile menu works

Title: Members Page Display
Preconditions: User logged in as company Admin or Maintainer, navigate to /company/members
Steps:
1. Log in as a company Admin or Maintainer
2. Navigate to /company/members (or Company > Members from account menu)
3. Verify the member list loads
4. Verify each member row displays: name, email, assigned role, account status (Active/Blocked)
Expected Result:
- /company/members page loads without error
- Member list displays all company members
- Each member row shows: full name, email address, role (Buyer/Admin), status
- List is sortable or filterable (if available)
- Own account visible in the list

Title: Invite Member - Buyer Role
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, on Members page
Steps:
1. Click 'Invite Member' button on the Members page
2. Enter recipient email address
3. Select role: 'Buyer'
4. Submit the invitation form
5. Verify success confirmation
6. Check that invitation appears as pending in the member list (if applicable)
Expected Result:
- 'Invite Member' button is visible for Admin users
- Invitation form accepts email address and role selection
- Role options include at minimum: Buyer, Admin
- After submission: success message 'Invitation sent' or similar
- Invited email receives an invitation email
- Pending invitation visible in member list (status: Invited/Pending)

Title: Invite Member - Admin Role
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, on Members page
Steps:
1. Click 'Invite Member'
2. Enter a new recipient email address
3. Select role: 'Admin'
4. Submit invitation
5. Recipient accepts invitation and logs in
6. Verify the new Admin has admin-level permissions (can invite others, can manage members)
Expected Result:
- Invitation with Admin role can be sent
- Invited Admin receives invitation email
- After accepting: new member appears in list with 'Admin' role
- New Admin can access member management and invitation features
- New Admin cannot exceed their own permission scope

Title: Edit Member Role
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, at least one Buyer member exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to /company/members
2. Locate a member with 'Buyer' role
3. Click Edit or role change option next to the member
4. Change role from 'Buyer' to 'Admin'
5. Save the change
6. Verify the member list updates immediately
7. Log in as the edited member and verify Admin permissions are active
Expected Result:
- Edit/role-change option available per member row
- Role dropdown or selector shows available roles
- Saving changes updates the member's role in the list immediately
- Changed member's permissions are updated on their next action or page load
- Member receives notification of role change (if configured)

Title: Block Member
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, at least one active Buyer member exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to /company/members
2. Locate an active Buyer member
3. Click Block or Deactivate option for that member
4. Confirm the block action in the dialog
5. Verify member status changes to 'Blocked' in the list
6. Attempt to log in as the blocked user
Expected Result:
- Block/Deactivate option available for non-Admin members
- Confirmation dialog appears: 'Block this member?'
- After confirmation: member status changes to 'Blocked' in the list
- Blocked member cannot sign in: receives error 'Your account has been blocked. Contact your administrator.'
- Blocked member's existing sessions invalidated (if applicable)

Title: Unblock Member
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, at least one Blocked member exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to /company/members
2. Locate a Blocked member
3. Click Unblock or Activate option for that member
4. Confirm the unblock action
5. Verify member status returns to 'Active'
6. Attempt to log in as the previously blocked member
Expected Result:
- Unblock/Activate option visible for Blocked members
- Confirmation dialog for unblock action (optional but expected)
- After unblock: member status returns to 'Active' in the list
- Previously blocked member can now sign in successfully
- Member's data and history preserved after unblock

Title: Filter Members by Role
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, member list has members with both Buyer and Admin roles
Steps:
1. Navigate to /company/members
2. Locate the filter control (dropdown, tabs, or checkbox)
3. Filter by role 'Buyer' and verify only Buyer members shown
4. Filter by role 'Admin' and verify only Admin members shown
5. Clear filter and verify all members shown
Expected Result:
- Role filter control is present on the Members page
- Filtering by 'Buyer' displays only members with Buyer role
- Filtering by 'Admin' displays only members with Admin role
- Member count updates to reflect the filter
- Clearing the filter restores the full member list

Title: Search Members
Preconditions: Logged in as company Admin, at least 5 members exist with distinct names
Steps:
1. Navigate to /company/members
2. Locate the search input field
3. Type part of a member's name (at least 3 characters)
4. Verify results filter dynamically as text is entered
5. Clear the search field
6. Verify full member list restored
Expected Result:
- Search input field is present on the Members page
- Typing filters the member list dynamically (live search or on Enter)
- Search matches on name and/or email address
- No-results state shown if no members match the search term
- Clearing search field restores the complete member list

Title: Impersonation - View as Customer
Preconditions: Admin account with impersonation permissions, target customer account exists
Steps:
1. Log in to Admin SPA
2. Navigate to customer account management
3. Select a target customer user
4. Initiate login-on-behalf (impersonation)
5. Observe the storefront session that opens
6. Verify catalog, prices, and cart reflect customer context
Expected Result:
- Storefront opens in customer context (not admin context)
- Catalog displays products available to that customer
- Prices shown match the customer pricing tier
- Customer cart is shown (not admin cart)
- No admin-only UI elements visible in storefront

Title: Impersonation - Switch Companies
Preconditions: Admin account with impersonation permissions, customer belongs to multiple companies
Steps:
1. Initiate impersonation of a customer who belongs to multiple companies
2. Observe the current company context
3. Use the company switcher in the storefront
4. Switch to a different company
5. Verify catalog, prices, and account context fully change
Expected Result:
- Company switcher accessible during impersonation session
- Switching company changes catalog and pricing context
- Account header reflects new company name
- Previously loaded company data does not bleed into new context
- No console errors on company switch

Title: Impersonation - Stop Impersonation
Steps:
1. While impersonating a customer, locate the stop-impersonation control
2. Click Stop Impersonation or equivalent
3. Observe the resulting session
4. Verify admin context is restored
Expected Result:
- Stop impersonation control visible and accessible during impersonation
- Clicking it ends the customer session
- Admin is returned to Admin SPA or admin-authenticated state
- No customer-specific data (cart, addresses, profile) persists in admin session
- Session cookies/tokens reflect admin identity after stop

Title: Impersonation - Place Order on Behalf
Preconditions: Admin impersonating a customer, cart accessible, checkout enabled
Steps:
1. Initiate impersonation of target customer
2. Add a product to cart
3. Proceed through checkout
4. Complete and place the order
5. Navigate to Order History in customer account
6. Verify order appears attributed to the customer
Expected Result:
- Checkout flow completes successfully during impersonation
- Order confirmation page shown
- Order appears in the customer Order History (not admin history)
- Order record shows customer as the owner
- Order can be found in Admin SPA under the customer account

Title: Impersonation - No Persistent Side Effects
Preconditions: Customer has items in cart before impersonation begins, admin account separate
Steps:
1. Note the customer cart contents before impersonation (via admin or pre-setup)
2. Admin initiates impersonation of the customer
3. Admin views the cart but does NOT modify it
4. Admin stops the impersonation session
5. Customer logs in independently
6. Customer verifies their cart is unchanged
Expected Result:
- Customer cart contents are identical before and after impersonation session
- No items added, removed, or quantity-changed by impersonation session
- Customer saved addresses unchanged
- Customer profile data unchanged
- No orphaned sessions remain after impersonation ends
